    Reymond Akpanya

    Postdoctoral Research Associate in Mathematics at the University of Sydney. Working with Prof. John Voight. Research in combinatorial topology, simplicial surfaces, and topological interlocking assemblies.

    • Sydney, Australia

    News

    Dec 2025 Talk at AustMS 2025, La Trobe University, Melbourne
    Sep 2025 Joined the Magma Group at the University of Sydney as a Magma Research Associate
    Jul 2025 Paper published in Journal of Algebraic Combinatorics: Surfaces with given automorphism group
    May 2025 PhD thesis defended: Constructing Symmetric Simplicial Surfaces, RWTH Aachen University

    I am a Magma Research Associate at the Magma Group, within the School of Mathematics and Statistics at the University of Sydney, working with John Voight.

    My research lies in combinatorics, computational algebra, and their applications, with a particular focus on the interplay between combinatorial structures and their geometric realisations as polyhedra. I combine theoretical methods with extensive use of computer algebra systems, in particular Magma, as well as GAP and related tools.

    Previously I completed my PhD thesis at RWTH Aachen University under the supervision of Alice C. Niemeyer and Wilhelm Plesken.

    Research

    Algebraic Graph Theory

    Simplicial Surfaces

    Group Actions on Discrete Structures

    Topological Interlockings
